ab: bulabulay mun, tucu a qadaw uri kacuin mun nua sinsin a sema qinaljan a kivala tua ramaljemaljeng.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: bulay yu! uri qivu amen tua kai a pinayuanan kata ramaljemaljeng.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說排灣語。
en: B: Okay! We have to speak Paiwanese to the seniors.

ab: uri djemaljun anga itjen?
en: A: Are we almost there?

ab: uri djemaljun anga.
en: B:Almost there.

ab: vuvu, mangetjengetjez amen a pacun tjanusun.
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: saigu mun a qivu ta pinayuanan a kai! aku kinananguaq!
ch: B:你們會說排灣語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B:You speak Paiwan! That’s great!

ab: bulabulay mun, tucu a qadaw uri kacuin mun nua sinsi a semapuvaquan a karakuda.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

ab: uii, sinsi.
en: B: Yes, sir.

ab: idu a mapuljat kasarisaringu, paqenetju a nunanemanemanga.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: uii, sinsi, nia inalap anga a penuljat a nia nemanema.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.

ab: djemaljun anga itjen?
en: A: Are we there yet?

ab: inianan a djemaljun, cemadjanan aravc.
en: B: Not yet, it’s still far away.

ab: tisunai a lemalja ta zaljum.
en: A: You go watering.

ab: uii, tiakenai a lemalja tazaljum.
en: B: Okay, I’ll water it.

ab: tisunai a malap ta pakazazaw.
en: A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: uii, tiaken a vaik a venilad taqayaqayam.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: bulabulai mun a mapuljat, tucu a qadaw uri vaik itjen a sema puqemuzimuzipan.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: kakubulai aravc! uri kemuda itjen a semazua?
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?

ab: paka basu itjen a vaik.
en: A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: kasarisaringu a mapuljat, uri venli itjen tua kipu.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

ab: uii.
en: B: Yes.

ab: a i tjaiqayaw pu qaqayaman, a i tjailikuz pu qatjuvian, pacunu a kisumadju.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

ab: sinsi marekutj aken ta qatjuvi.
en: B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: iniamakuda, sau pacunu numaya ta qayaqayam.
en: A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ab: ucevucevungi anga imaza nu itanga tukian/milingan.
ch: A:我們一個小時後回來集合。
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.

ab: uii, masalu sinsi.
en: B: Yes, thank you.

ab: anema su pinacunan tucu a qadaw?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: napacu aken ta liyaw aravc a qatjuvi kata liyaw aravac a qaqayam.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.

ab: kadjaljavu, uri vaik anga itjen.
en: A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: kaljavaw aken a palamu, mazeli anga ku kula.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: bulay a vaik itjen a ljemangui?
en: A: Shall we go swimming together?

ab: bulay aravc, pai ari vaik!
en: B:Yes! Let’s go!

ab: tjuruvu aravac a caucau a ljemanguljanguy imaza.
en: A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: vacan azua izua, bulai a tjaljumaza itjen a ljemangui.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
